label: Uncomplicated bursitis without septic-joint or trauma concern
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated bursitis.
No septic joint or infection concern, open wound, trauma/fracture concern, neurovascular compromise, inability to use limb, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Septic joint concern, red/hot joint, fever, cellulitis, abscess, open wound, systemic illness, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, inability to use limb, severe pain, or high-energy trauma.
Immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, occupational health, or orthopedics if swelling or function is not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports bursitis framing as inflammation of a bursa with joint pain/tenderness/swelling and supportive care such as rest, avoiding pressure, and clinician-directed pain control. Autonomous Phase 1851-1900 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated bursitis without septic joint/infection concern, open wound, trauma/fracture concern, neurovascular compromise, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports bursitis framing as inflammation of a bursa with joint pain/tenderness/swelling and supportive care such as rest, avoiding pressure, and clinician-directed pain control. Autonomous Phase 1851-1900 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated bursitis without septic joint/infection concern, open wound, trauma/fracture concern, neurovascular compromise, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated bursitis
no septic joint/infection concern, trauma, or neurovascular red flags documented
no open wound, high-risk host, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
